meta.description: Curated CLI Matrix registry for CLI-Hub multi-CLI workflows (capability-based, v2 schema)

matrices.name: video-creation
matrices.description: Capability-based matrix for end-to-end video production: storyboard planning, story/audio direction, internet video/music search/download, capture, generation, voice/music, footage analysis, transcription, high-end caption design, assembly, overlay, thumbnail, encode, and quality review. Providers include harness CLIs, public CLIs, Python libs, native binaries, agent skills, and cloud APIs.

capabilities.intent: Turn a brief into global creative direction, narrative/emotional arc, audio arc, script, shot list, timing map, storyboard, and asset requirements before production. Default to agent-native planning unless a focused external skill is clearly worth installing.

providers.name: Agent-native planning
providers.notes: Default path for creative_direction.md, story arc, emotional curve, audio arc, beat map, scripts, shot lists, timing maps, storyboards, and asset plans; read cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/story-structure-audio.md for non-trivial videos.

providers.name: storyboard-creation skill
providers.notes: Install with `pnpm dlx add-skill https://github.com/inference-sh/skills/tree/HEAD/guides/video/storyboard-creation`. Use only for shot grammar, camera angles, continuity, storyboard panels, and animatic planning.

providers.name: remotion-best-practices skill
providers.notes: Install with `npx skills add https://github.com/remotion-dev/skills --skill remotion-best-practices`; SKILL.md: https://github.com/remotion-dev/skills/blob/main/skills/remotion/SKILL.md. Use when the storyboard will become Remotion/React motion-video code.

capabilities.intent: Discover candidate internet footage, stock clips, public-domain clips, or platform URLs before download.

providers.name: Web search + source filters
providers.notes: Use site/source filters for YouTube, Bilibili, Wikimedia Commons, stock sites, and public-source searches; record URL, creator, license, and attribution before editing.

capabilities.intent: Download or import selected web video URLs into the workspace with provenance metadata.

providers.name: yt-dlp
providers.notes: General downloader for YouTube, Bilibili, and many sites; supports search, playlists, cookies, and quality filters.

providers.name: you-get
providers.notes: Multi-site media downloader with Bilibili support.

providers.name: lux
providers.notes: Go CLI downloader with Bilibili, YouTube, Douyin, Youku, and other site support.

providers.name: BBDown
providers.notes: Bilibili-focused CLI; useful for subtitles, danmaku, playlists, favorites, and high-quality streams.

capabilities.intent: Discover existing songs, BGM, soundtrack cues, platform audio, or specific music versions before download.

providers.name: Web search + music source filters
providers.notes: Search for official artist/label uploads, official audio/MV, verified lyric videos, royalty-free sources, or the exact requested cover/version; record rights and attribution metadata.

providers.name: yt-dlp search extractors
providers.notes: Use ytsearch/scsearch only after the query and version constraints are clear; inspect candidates before download.

providers.name: spotdl metadata/search
providers.notes: Useful when the user names a Spotify track/playlist and metadata alignment matters; respect platform rights and user authorization.

capabilities.intent: Download or import existing music into the workspace with provenance metadata, conversion, and source-audio validation.

providers.name: yt-dlp audio download
providers.notes: General audio download/extraction path for many supported sites. For Bilibili, download raw m4a and convert with ffmpeg instead of `yt-dlp -x --audio-format mp3`; verify mean volume after conversion.

providers.name: spotdl
providers.notes: Downloads tracks/playlists with Spotify metadata and matched audio sources. Use only when the user is authorized to use the resulting music.

providers.name: scdl
providers.notes: SoundCloud-focused downloader for public/authorized SoundCloud tracks.

providers.name: bandcamp-dl
providers.notes: Bandcamp-focused downloader for public or user-owned Bandcamp tracks/albums.

providers.name: local file import + ffmpeg
providers.notes: Use when the user supplies local music files; normalize/convert only after validating the correct version and clean audio.

capabilities.intent: Record screen, webcam, or application windows to a video file.
Produce a video clip from a text prompt or reference image.

providers.name: jimeng
providers.notes: ByteDance Dreamina; shares model family with Seedance.

providers.name: Runway Gen-4
providers.notes: ~$0.05/sec, cinematic motion.

providers.name: Seedance
providers.notes: ByteDance Seedance; realistic motion.

capabilities.intent: Record, clean, trim, normalize, or denoise audio tracks.
Generate speech audio from text (narration / voiceover).
Generate background music or sound effects that follow the planned audio arc rather than a flat continuous bed.
Segment, label, OCR, and index video footage so an editor can find usable shots across many clips.

providers.name: PySceneDetect scenedetect
providers.notes: Fast local scene cut detection; can save scene lists and start/mid/end keyframes.

providers.name: Google Cloud Video Intelligence
providers.notes: Use for labels, people, objects, text, speech, and shot-change annotations when cloud processing is acceptable.

providers.name: TwelveLabs video search/index
providers.notes: Use for semantic search over larger footage libraries and multimodal visual/audio indexing.

providers.name: PaddleOCR on sampled keyframes
providers.notes: Run after keyframe extraction to index visible text, signage, lower thirds, and hard subtitles; it complements scene detection rather than replacing it.

capabilities.intent: Transcribe speech to text and produce subtitle files (SRT/ASS).

providers.name: cli-anything-videocaptioner
providers.notes: Can use cloud ASR backends if configured.

providers.name: openai-whisper
providers.notes: First use may download model weights. Prefer small/base CPU models for drafts; use larger models only when runtime/resources fit the task.

providers.name: faster-whisper/stable-ts
providers.notes: Package availability does not guarantee model cache or acceptable runtime. Choose model size by quality/time constraints.

capabilities.intent: Design, time, render, and QC visible captions, subtitles, lyrics, karaoke, lower thirds, and trailer title hits so they look polished and genre-appropriate.

providers.name: Captions reference module
providers.notes: Read cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/captions.md. Use for polished visible captions, subtitles, lyrics, lower thirds, genre presets, safe-zone rules, and caption QC.

providers.name: ASS + ffmpeg subtitles
providers.notes: Deterministic local path for styled subtitle burn-in on edited footage; supply fontsdir when custom fonts are used.

providers.name: HyperFrames captions workflow
providers.notes: Install with `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --skill hyperframes`. Use only when captions are part of an HTML/CSS/GSAP motion composition, karaoke, audio-reactive typography, or digital launch video.

providers.name: pysubs2 ASS authoring
providers.notes: Programmatically author ASS events/styles while keeping final burn-in deterministic through ffmpeg.

providers.name: MoviePy/Pillow transparent overlays
providers.notes: Use only when custom layout exceeds ASS and the project already uses a Python render path; inspect caption-heavy frames before delivery.

capabilities.intent: Assemble clips on a timeline with transitions, following the creative direction, story arc, source roles, and audio plan.

providers.name: HyperFrames skill/CLI
providers.notes: Install skill with `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes`; CLI can also run via `npx hyperframes preview/render`. Use only for website/product-page or digital UI-driven launch videos where HTML/CSS/GSAP composition is appropriate, not as a general NLE or Remotion/React path.

capabilities.intent: Apply already-designed caption/subtitle assets, watermarks, or picture-in-picture layers onto a video.
Create a thumbnail image or social card.
Final mux / codec / container for the output video.
Check rendered video for technical, editorial, story-structure, and audio defects before delivery.

providers.name: ffmpeg/ffprobe QC filters
providers.notes: Primary local QC path for metadata, black frames, silence, freezes, crop/aspect checks, and LUFS/loudness analysis.

providers.name: MediaInfo CLI
providers.notes: Use for independent container/codec/bitrate/duration/aspect-ratio metadata checks.

providers.name: ffmpeg-quality-metrics / VMAF
providers.notes: Install with `pipx install ffmpeg-quality-metrics`; source: https://github.com/slhck/ffmpeg-quality-metrics. Use VMAF/SSIM/PSNR only when a reference video exists.

recipes.description: Record a screen walkthrough with narration and subtitles.
Audio-first, visualize and caption for video platforms.
Find internet clips, download them with provenance, then edit a montage.
Build a music video around a user-specified or discovered existing song.
Product/site-driven launch video with animated UI, typography, and motion graphics.

known_gaps.reason: No first-party or public CLI for YouTube/TikTok/Bilibili/Instagram yet.
known_gaps.workaround: Instruct the user to upload manually via the web UI, or escalate to a custom script using each platform's v3 API with an OAuth token the user supplies.

known_gaps.reason: Sota-tier cinematic output is only available via paid APIs (Runway, Kling, Seedance).
known_gaps.workaround: Use generate-veo-video or jimeng for high-quality output when configured; avoid pulling complex local GPU/weights stacks into the matrix by default.

known_gaps.reason: No automated license/TOS/provenance verifier for web-sourced video or music.
known_gaps.workaround: Save sources.json/music_sources.json with source URL, creator, license, intended use, and attribution text; ask the user before using unclear or restricted media.

known_gaps.reason: External agent skills are reported as agent-installable instead of available/missing because their local install state is not machine-checkable by the env/binary/package preflight contract.
known_gaps.workaround: Use provider notes and the SKILL.md source/install table to install or inspect the external skill before relying on it.

matrices.suggest_to_user_template: To enable <capability> via <provider>, please set <ENV_VAR>. Cost: <cost_tier>. Quality: <quality_tier>. Reply 'skip' to fall back to <next provider>.
